title: sida pedunculata
intro: Sida pedunculata, also known as the "country mallow" or "ball mallow," is a plant species native to tropical Africa and Asia, belonging to the Malvaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Sida pedunculata include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, and its leaves and roots are commonly used in traditional remedies.
